ops.text: SECURITY FIND (concurrent-session collision, not our regression): captain-hook main commit b65dfe88 "refactor(deletions): rewrite rm guard onto fluent evt.cmd API" (pushed by another session minutes after v11.0.0 tagged) shipped 7 fail-open/over-block regressions in the rm safety guard — masked until now because it also broke test collection (ImportError rm_targets), so the 8 dormant tests never ran. Contradicted its own commit message's "fails closed on unverified targets" claim. Tests green on parent 0c9f5d9e = the spec. MITIGATION: v11.0.0 was tagged BEFORE b65dfe88, so no released wheel regressed — main-only, must be green before any next tag. Fix lane (fable, security-sensitive impl per Models table; NOT opus) on branch fix-deletions-import: 7e6c478b (test import align) + 17a44d63 (restore 3 dropped decision points: unverified-target literal reclassification, emittable() re-emission gate reused from util/shell.emit_token, backslash-newline splice forfeit) + 62221aff (test-8 glob-internals re-point to util.globbing). test_pack_deletions 86 green, cmd+packs 121 green, ruff clean. PLUS a pre-existing fail-open the lane found and I authorized fixing in-branch: bare command substitution `rm $(ls)`/`rm \`ls\`` dispatched to ALLOW (call.targets empty → check never consults targets.complete) — fix = fail closed when not call.targets.complete + inline rows. Deferred (task #17): move backslash-newline forfeit from pack-local into cmd.py Call.spliceable (deletions is sub()'s only consumer today). NEXT: codex security-verify over the complete 4-commit diff (codex usage limit cleared — probe returned clean), then push to main, then it unblocks the captain-hook red + any future release.
